heroHeadline: Somalia: Parliament Expired Apr 14, Puntland Declares Constitutional Vacuum; 54 Al-Shabaab Killed in Juba Airstrikes; Gorgor Forces Deployed as Presidential Term Enters Final 27 Days
heroSubtitle: Somalia's constitutional crisis deepened as the Federal Parliament's mandate expired April 14 without elections — Speaker Madobe's unilateral one-year extension was rejected by Puntland, Jubbaland, and the opposition. On April 17, Puntland formally declared a 'constitutional vacuum' and urged President Mohamud to negotiate before his own term expires May 15. On the security front, SNA and Jubbaland forces killed 54 Al-Shabaab fighters in Juba regions airstrikes (Apr 15-16) and another 27 in ground operations (Apr 18), while NISA eliminated 9 militants in Lower Shabelle. Turkish-trained Gorgor special forces were deployed around a Mogadishu military camp April 18 amid fears of internal military fracturing. Diplomatically, Somalia condemned Israel's appointment of an ambassador to Somaliland and issued an extraordinary threat of Bab el-Mandeb restrictions — dismissed as unenforceable but flagged by analysts as risking alignment with Houthi forces. Turkey's Çağrı Bey drillship has been drilling the 'Curad-1' well off Somalia's coast since April 10; a Somali senator labeled Turkey 'resources pirates' over undisclosed TPAO concession terms.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
